turns.user_message: Yesterday evening, I sat on the couch and noticed how quiet the room felt when the door finally clicked shut. A few months ago, that kind of quiet used to feel completely normal, but now it just sits heavy on my shoulders. I tried to start a casual conversation about the weather, but the words felt completely hollow. I ended up just staring at the wall while the minutes ticked by. It is strange how a simple moment of stillness can suddenly feel so exhausting.

Lately, that same heavy quiet has been showing up almost every single time we share a space. I catch myself overthinking every little gesture, wondering if I am the reason the air feels so thick. I used to just brush off these awkward pauses, but now they linger in my mind long after the moment passes. I find myself rehearsing simple things to say, only to swallow them back down. It feels like I am constantly walking on eggshells, waiting for the other shoe to drop.

Now, that lingering silence has completely taken over my headspace, and I do not know how to shake it loose. Every shared glance feels loaded with unspoken weight, and I am starting to dread the moments when we are in the same room. I keep replaying yesterday’s quiet evening on a loop, wondering when everything shifted so drastically. The distance between us feels impossible to bridge, no matter how hard I try to fill the empty space. I just feel completely drained by the constant effort of pretending everything is fine.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This used to not bother the speaker.
